seoTitle: Mount Wyatt vs Mountain Camp — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mount Wyatt and Mountain Camp. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mount Wyatt vs Mountain Camp: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mount Wyatt and Mountain Camp.
heading: Mount Wyatt vs Mountain Camp — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mount Wyatt, Queensland and Mountain Camp, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mount Wyatt has a lower overall climate risk score (28/100) compared to Mountain Camp (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mount Wyatt or Mountain Camp safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mount Wyatt has a flood risk score of 26/100, while Mountain Camp scores 49/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mount Wyatt scores 23/100 for bushfire risk. Mountain Camp scores 45/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mount Wyatt or Mountain Camp?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mount Wyatt has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.8% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
